probes.name: digest_crossref_goals_projects
probes.prompt: Hey, I need a research digest. I've been working on my master thesis about data warehouse architectures and also setting up a product website for Prostownik with renders of variants. Can you gather any new knowledge diffs from the last few days that are relevant to either of these, cross-reference them with my goals, and send me a concise digest with at most 5 actionable items? Things I should check, try, watch, or read. Send it to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com
probes.criteria:
The support/research_digest agent must produce a concise digest that (1) identifies knowledge diffs relevant to BOTH the data warehouse/master thesis topic AND the Prostownik product/website project, (2) cross-references findings against the stated goals, (3) outputs at most 5 concrete actionable items categorized as check/try/watch/read, and (4) confirms sending to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com. A good response does NOT produce more than 5 items and each item is specific enough to act on immediately.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #9 (Prostownik renders, website setup) and #21 (data warehouse thesis content) — the user frequently juggles multiple projects and expects the agent to cross-reference.

probes.name: polish_tech_research_summary
probes.prompt: Zbierz mi digest z najnowszych informacji o konfiguracji Qwen 3.6-35B na urządzeniach typu Halo Strix i podobnych. Mam projekt związany z fine-tuningiem modeli i chcę wiedzieć co nowego się pojawiło. Skróć to do max 5 punktów action items — co sprawdzić, przetestować, poczytać. Wyślij na mój email ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com
probes.criteria:
The support/research_digest agent must respond in or acknowledge Polish, gather recent knowledge diffs about configuring Qwen 3.6-35B on Halo Strix or similar hardware, cross-reference with the user's fine-tuning project context, produce at most 5 actionable items (check/try/watch/read) in a concise digest format, and confirm email delivery to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com. A good response is specific about Qwen config approaches, not generic ML advice.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: watch_digest_ongoing_topics
probes.prompt: Can you prepare a weekly-style research digest for me? Here are the things I'm tracking right now: 1) master thesis competitions for best thesis in Poland (especially ones organized by ABW and similar institutions, next editions for 2026), 2) any new commits or changes in the TechTree codebase that relate to my work, 3) data quality and closed-loop analysis methods. Cross-reference whatever you find with these active projects of mine and give me up to 5 things I should actually do — check, try, watch, or read. Keep it tight.
probes.criteria:
The support/research_digest agent must (1) acknowledge all three tracking topics (master thesis competitions in Poland, TechTree repo changes, data quality/closed-loop analysis), (2) cross-reference findings with the user's stated active projects, (3) produce at most 5 actionable items in check/try/watch/read format, and (4) keep the digest concise. A good response addresses each topic area and does NOT exceed 5 items total.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #10 (master thesis competitions, ABW), #12 (TechTree codebase changes), #6 (closed-loop analysis for data quality), and #22 (in-depth commit analysis).

probes.name: quick_actionable_snapshot
probes.prompt: Potrzebuję szybkiego digestu. Sprawdź czy są jakieś nowe rzeczy do których powinienem zwrócić uwagę związane z: moją obroną pracy magisterskiej (tematy: hurtownie danych, jakość danych, bezpieczeństwo informacji), moimi fakturami które muszę zarejestrować do 10-go czerwca, i czymkolwiek nowym o unsloth lub fine-tuning. Max 5 action items, krótko i na temat.

The support/research_digest agent must (1) address all three areas: master thesis defense topics (data warehouses, data quality, information security), invoice registration deadline around June 10th, and unsloth/fine-tuning developments, (2) cross-reference with the user's known goals and deadlines, (3) output at most 5 concise actionable items, and (4) respond in Polish or acknowledge the Polish request. If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #13 (invoices up to 10th), #18 (invoice registration system), #9 (unsloth done, fine-tuning), and #19 (master thesis defense preparation topics).

probes.name: email_digest_multi_source
probes.prompt: I need you to compile a digest pulling from everything you can find — my recent research threads, any emails on TechTree or the repo that need my attention, and new developments in applying closed-loop analysis to data quality in data warehouses. Cross-reference all of it with my ongoing goals: finishing the master thesis defense prep, getting Prostownik product renders done for the website, and learning the new invoice registration system. Give me at most 5 things to check, try, watch or read. Send the digest to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com please.
probes.criteria:
The support/research_digest agent must (1) pull from multiple sources (research threads, TechTree emails/repo, closed-loop analysis/data quality developments), (2) cross-reference against three explicit goals (thesis defense, Prostownik renders, invoice system learning), (3) produce at most 5 prioritized actionable items in check/try/watch/read format, (4) keep the digest concise, and (5) confirm email delivery to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com. TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #6 (closed-loop data quality), #9 (Prostownik renders), #18 (invoice registration system), #19 (thesis defense), and #27 (check emails on TechTree repo).
